repos_compared.name: Depth anything 3-File Content
repos_compared.type: research-oriented Python package for monocular and multi-view 3D inference
repos_compared.primary_flow: ENTRY -> interface -> api/service orchestration -> model inference -> export/backend/streaming output

repos_compared.name: Qwen-image-layered File Content
repos_compared.primary_flow: ENTRY -> decompose image -> hand off RGBA artifacts -> optional layer editing -> recomposition/export

repos_compared.name: Sam3_File Contents
repos_compared.type: layered research/ML package with factory-driven model construction and optional agent orchestration
repos_compared.primary_flow: ENTRY -> model factory/predictor setup -> image or video segmentation runtime -> optional agent loop -> evaluation or visualization output

shared_patterns: Python-first ML repositories with GPU-oriented runtime assumptions
User-facing execution surfaces separated from heavy model logic
Artifact-oriented outputs beyond raw tensor predictions
Prepared analyses emphasize workflows and operator usability
key_differences: Depth Anything 3 is the most platform-like with API, CLI, UI, backend, and streaming surfaces around one core
Qwen-image-layered is the most workflow-centric and least modular, coupling stages through files and user actions
SAM 3 is the most library-like in core modeling and factory assembly, with an adjacent agent layer rather than multiple operator UIs

operator_interfaces.reason: It centralizes one inference core behind multiple coherent surfaces rather than duplicating workflow logic.

workflow_clarity.reason: Its stage-by-stage producer/edit/recompose design is immediately legible to operators.

model_assembly_and_reuse.reason: The factory-driven builder and predictor split suggests stronger internal composability for model variants.

artifact_export_and_downstream_interop.reason: It provides the richest export surface across visualization, reconstruction, and machine-readable outputs.

evaluation_and_validation_surfaces.reason: It exposes the strongest explicit evaluation and benchmarking layer in the available analyses.

repo_alignment.Depth anything 3-File Content: CLI/API/UI/backend/streaming -> api/service orchestration -> DA3 model and geometry stack -> export/backend/streamed artifacts
repo_alignment.Qwen-image-layered File Content: Gradio scripts -> stage-specific app logic -> image decomposition/edit/composition pipelines -> layer files and creative exports
repo_alignment.Sam3_File Contents: README/scripts/factory entry -> model_builder/predictor/agent control -> segmentation and SAM core modules -> masks, scores, visualizations, eval artifacts

alignment_notes: All three repos follow a clear staged flow even though only Depth Anything 3 and SAM 3 exhibit deep internal layering.
Qwen-image-layered aligns at the workflow level, but its control and logic are more collapsed into scripts.

conflicts.details: Qwen-image-layered is script-first, while Depth Anything 3 and SAM 3 favor reusable package structures.
Qwen-image-layered uses manual artifact handoffs; Depth Anything 3 and SAM 3 center reusable Python contracts.
Depth Anything 3 emphasizes many operator interfaces, SAM 3 emphasizes model/runtime assembly, and Qwen-image-layered emphasizes a guided creative workflow.
All three depend on heavyweight model assets and GPUs, but Qwen-image-layered also lacks a clear dependency manifest in the analyzed snapshot.

integration_opportunities: Adopt SAM 3 style factory/builder separation for heavyweight model assembly in repos that currently blend orchestration and runtime concerns.
Adopt Depth Anything 3 style central API contract so multiple UX surfaces can share one implementation core.
Adopt Qwen-image-layered's explicit stage framing for operator-facing workflows, but back it with typed internal contracts instead of manual file handoffs.
Borrow SAM 3's evaluation-script emphasis to make runtime and quality validation first-class in the other repos.
Borrow Depth Anything 3's export/interoperability orientation when designing downstream artifact pipelines.

recommended_flow: controller resolves workflow and validates inputs
factory assembles or selects the model runtime
core logic executes inference or transformation
evaluation or telemetry layer records quality/runtime signals
best_practices: Keep one authoritative inference core and fan out UX layers around it.
Use factories and predictors to isolate model construction from request handling.
Prefer explicit handoff contracts over implicit shared state, but automate them instead of relying on manual operator transfer.
Treat export, evaluation, and interoperability as first-class modules rather than afterthoughts.
Design optional advanced subsystems such as streaming or agents as extensions over stable core contracts.

rationale: Pattern consistency is strong across the available foundation, architecture, and explanation documents.
Flow alignment is high at the abstract pipeline level.
Confidence is reduced by snapshot-based analysis and incomplete operational/dependency details in some repos.

unknowns: No raw-code verification was performed by design; findings rely only on prepared analysis outputs.
Deployment, CI, and production hardening details are mostly absent across the compared inputs.
Some SAM 3 imports appear to reference files not present in the extracted snapshot.
The Qwen-image-layered snapshot is very small and may omit packaging or support files.
